analysis_result: Fisher's exact p-value = 0.3147 (OR=5.00) for SHH pathway alterations (PTCH1 loss OR SMO gain) in MPNST-G1 (5/8 = 62.5%) vs MPNST-G2 (2/8 = 25.0%) using CONUMEE-style CNV calls (log2 ratio thresholds: loss < -0.15, gain > +0.15) at ±5Mb regions around PTCH1 (chr9:98,205,264-98,279,247) and SMO (chr7:128,828,690-128,855,823). This does not reach the paper-reported 75% vs 12.5%; if those exact proportions (6/8 vs 1/8) were applied, Fisher's p = 0.0406 (OR=21.0). The discrepancy likely reflects differences in baseline correction (pooled vs normal-tissue reference) and CNV call thresholds vs. the authors' CONUMEE implementation in R.
denominator_used: N=16 MPNST samples with methylation IDAT data (8 MPNST-G1 + 8 MPNST-G2); denominator per group = 8 each, from clinical data file 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx restricted to Methylation_Group in {MPNST_G1, MPNST_G2}
assumptions_made: Used methylprep Python library (no conumee R) as a CNV substitute; processed all 108 EPIC IDATs to compute total intensity per probe (Type II = grnA+redA; Type I Red = redA+redB; Type I Grn = grnA+grnB); normalized sample-wise by sample median log2 intensity then subtracted the across-sample median per probe as diploid reference; aggregated probes within ±5Mb of PTCH1 (chr9:98,205,264-98,279,247, hg19) and SMO (chr7:128,828,690-128,855,823, hg19) by median log2 ratio; called PTCH1 loss if region log2 < -0.15 and SMO gain if region log2 > +0.15; defined SHH pathway alteration as PTCH1 loss OR SMO gain; used a pooled reference (median across all 108 samples) rather than a dedicated normal-tissue reference because no separate normal panel was provided; used hg19 coordinates (consistent with EPIC manifest Genome_Build=37)
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED: (1) Clinical sheet 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx, 108 rows with Sentrix_ID and Methylation_Group; (2) EPIC v1.0 manifest GPL21145_MethylationEPIC_15073387_v-1-0.csv.gz with 868,565 probes (IlmnID, AddressA_ID, AddressB_ID, Infinium_Design_Type, Color_Channel, CHR, MAPINFO); (3) 216 IDATs (108 Grn + 108 Red) from GSE207207_idats/.

B. COHORT DEFINITION: Filter 1 = Sentrix_ID present -> 108; Filter 2 = Methylation_Group in {MPNST_G1, MPNST_G2} -> 16 (G1=8, G2=8). All 16 had IDAT files matched by Sentrix_ID.

C. VARIABLE CODING: PTCH1 region = chr9:[98,205,264-5e6, 98,279,247+5e6] = chr9:[93,205,264-103,279,247] -> 3,147 EPIC probes. SMO region = chr7:[128,828,690-5e6, 128,855,823+5e6] = chr7:[123,828,690-133,855,823] -> 2,749 EPIC probes. PTCH1_loss := region_log2_ratio < -0.15; SMO_gain := region_log2_ratio > +0.15; SHH_alt := PTCH1_loss OR SMO_gain.

D. COMPUTATION: (1) For each sample (n=108): read Grn and Red IDATs with methylprep.files.IdatDataset, obtain mean_value per illumina AddressID; (2) per-probe total intensity: Type II = grnA + redA; Type I Red = redA + redB; Type I Grn = grnA + grnB; (3) log2(intensity+1); (4) sample-wise center by subtracting per-sample median log2 across all probes (Tukey-style); (5) compute per-probe median across all 108 samples as pooled-diploid reference; (6) log2_ratio = sample-centered - probe reference; (7) per-sample summary: median log2_ratio across PTCH1 region probes and across SMO region probes; (8) apply call thresholds; (9) 2x2 contingency MPNST-G1 vs MPNST-G2 by any-SHH-alteration; (10) scipy.stats.fisher_exact two-sided.

MPNST-G1 per-sample ptch1_log2, smo_log2: M1014 (-0.019, +0.146); M2252 (-0.080, +0.208); M2346 (-0.275, -0.287); M2372 (-0.166, +0.176); M3048 (+0.211, +0.322); M3398 (+0.007, +0.072); M6712 (-0.023, +0.293); M803 (-0.134, +0.121). PTCH1_loss calls: M2346, M2372. SMO_gain calls: M1014, M2252, M2372, M3048, M6712. Any-alteration calls: M2252, M2346, M2372, M3048, M6712 -> 5/8.

MPNST-G2 per-sample ptch1_log2, smo_log2: M1677 (-0.018, -0.039); M1933 (+0.005, +0.015); M2325 (+0.017, -0.000); M2377 (+0.027, -0.010); M2491 (+0.065, +0.161); M4719 (+0.039, +0.104); M9413 (+0.103, -0.001); M9450 (-0.228, -0.037). PTCH1_loss: M9450. SMO_gain: M2491. Any-alteration: M2491, M9450 -> 2/8.

Contingency table [[G1+, G1-],[G2+, G2-]] = [[5, 3],[2, 6]]. scipy.stats.fisher_exact -> OR=5.00, two-sided p=0.3147.

E. RESULT: Fisher's exact p = 0.3147 (OR=5.00); G1 62.5% vs G2 25.0% altered. The paper-reported 75% vs 12.5% (6/8 vs 1/8) would give Fisher's p=0.0406; our Python methylprep substitute underestimates the paper's G1-SHH rate and slightly overestimates G2 (one extra G2 call at M2491 which has modest SMO region gain of +0.161).

F. CAVEATS: (1) No dedicated non-neoplastic reference panel was used; the pooled 108-sample median serves as baseline, which can regress tumor-specific CNVs toward zero when many samples share alterations, reducing apparent effect size. (2) Sensitivity at thresholds ±0.10 gave G1 7/8 (87.5%) vs G2 3/8 (37.5%), p=0.119; ±0.20 gave G1 4/8 (50%) vs G2 1/8 (12.5%), p=0.282. Exact paper match (6/8 vs 1/8) was not reproduced at any symmetric threshold with the pooled-reference approach. (3) CONUMEE in R uses a curated control set and excludes variable probes; the Python methylprep substitute does not replicate that exactly. (4) A ±500kb focused region at threshold ±0.15 gave G1 6/8 (75%, matching paper numerator) but G2 3/8 (37.5%); no configuration yielded G2=1/8 exactly. (5) Denominator N=8 per group gives very low statistical power; the small-sample uncertainty is large.
